title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hair fall

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with hair fall because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy follicle function. The antioxidants in rosemary also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hair loss. Additionally, rosemary's natural astringent properties help to tighten the hair follicles, allowing them to produce stronger, healthier hair growth. By using rosemary tinctures, individuals can nourish their scalps and promote a fuller, thicker head of hair over time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds help protect the scalp and hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause hair loss and thinning by restricting blood flow to the hair follicles.
Stimulant property: Rosemary's stimulatory properties help increase circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpene has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce hair fall by protecting the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting hair growth and strengthening hair follicles.

Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing hair fall.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help promote hair growth and reduce shedding.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, an anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce dandruff and promote a healthy scalp.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help stimulate hair growth and reduce hair fall due to oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems by cutting them during peak growth season.
Dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems in a single layer at room temperature for 1 week.
Combine 1 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis mixture in the vodka for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with hair fall because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin helps to reduce inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth by blocking DHT production, a common contributor to hair loss. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to repair damaged hair follicles, strengthening them and reducing breakage.
remedy_properties: Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Turmeric's COX-inhibiting property helps to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory hormones that damage hair follicles.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to hair follicles, leading to hair fall, promoting healthy hair growth instead.

Curcumin's anti-androgenic effects: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss by causing the shrinkage and eventual death of hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce hair fall by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the growth of hair follicles.
Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has antioxidant properties that help protect hair follicles from oxidative damage, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used in tinctures to treat hair fall due to their ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce dandruff, thereby promoting healthy hair growth.

(Optional, not part of the original top 3, but still a commonly used part) Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa are sometimes used in tinctures to treat hair fall due to their potential to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried curcuma longa roots by cutting them during the peak of the plant's growth.
Chop the curcuma longa roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of 80% ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for the roots' active compounds to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American ginseng tinctures helps with hair fall because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. Ginseng also improves blood circulation, increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the hair follicles, promoting healthy growth and reducing the likelihood of breakage. Additionally, it has been shown to stimulate hair growth hormones, such as DHT, while also calming the scalp and reducing inflammation, resulting in a thicker, healthier head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: American Ginseng's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hair loss by protecting the scalp from damage caused by free radicals.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in American Ginseng tincture can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation, which can lead to hair thinning or loss by calming the immune response and reducing damage to hair follicles.

Immunomodulatory Effects: American Ginseng's immunomodulatory properties may help regulate the immune system's response to hair follicle damage, promoting a healthy balance between hair growth and shedding.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins help with hair fall by promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss.

Quercetin: A type of flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Notoginsenoside R1: This ginsenoside has been shown to stimulate hair growth by promoting the proliferation of hair follicle cells and reducing the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in ginsenosides, which have been shown to promote hair growth and reduce hair loss.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and other compounds that may help to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
Barks: They have been traditionally used to treat various hair and scalp issues, including hair loss, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 to 200 grams of panax quinquefolius roots from mature plants in the fall season.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter for 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with hair fall because it contains aloin, a compound that strengthens the roots of hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff, common causes of hair loss. Additionally, aloe's antioxidant properties help to repair damaged hair cuticles, reducing breakage and frizz. By nourishing the scalp and hair strands, herbal aloe tinctures can help to slow down hair fall and promote thicker, healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss by blocking the follicles' ability to grow new hair.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to hair loss, promoting a healthy scalp environment for hair growth.

Stimulates blood flow: Herbal aloe tinctures can help stimulate blood flow to the scalp, which increases oxygen and nutrient delivery to the hair follicles, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing the risk of hair fall.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone derivative that helps reduce hair fall by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that promotes hair loss.
Aloin: Aloin is a glycoside that helps reduce hair fall by increasing blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth, and reducing inflammation that can contribute to hair loss.

Saponins: Saponins are a group of compounds that help reduce hair fall by cleansing the scalp, removing dirt and other impurities that can clog hair follicles and prevent hair growth, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in aloin, a compound that helps to reduce hair fall by promoting hair growth and strengthening hair follicles.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain vitamins and minerals like vitamin E and calcium, which help to nourish and protect the scalp, reducing hair fall.
Flowers: The flowers of Aloe vera are known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce dandruff and scalp irritation, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe vera leaves by selecting those with firm fleshy texture and vibrant green color.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and weigh out 1 pound for a typical batch size.
Combine the aloe vera pieces with 5 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tinctures helps with hair fall because they are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth. The bioactive compounds present in herbal tea tinctures such as rosemary, saw palmetto, and biotin stimulate blood flow to the scalp, strengthening hair follicles and preventing hair loss. Additionally, these tinctures help to regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce inflammation, and soothe an itchy scalp, all of which contribute to a healthy and full head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal tea tinctures, such as those made from green tea or ginkgo biloba, contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can damage hair follicles and contribute to hair loss.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tinctures like saw palmetto or rosemary contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can help promote healthy hair growth by reducing irritation and redness.
Stimulatory effects: Herbal tea tinctures like ashwagandha or horsetail stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by delivering oxygen and nutrients to the follicles.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, especially epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce hair fall by protecting hair follicles from damage and promoting hair growth.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, formed during the fermentation process, have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to hair loss, thereby reducing hair fall.

Quinic acid derivatives (Quinic acid and Quercetin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce hair fall by protecting hair follicles from damage, promoting hair growth, and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
Flowers: Camellia sinensis flowers are used to create a hair tonic that helps to strengthen hair follicles and promote a healthy scalp.
Roots: Camellia sinensis roots are used to create a tincture that helps to nourish and stimulate hair growth, while also reducing hair fall and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves and 750 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the camellia sinensis leaves and vodka in the glass jar, seal it tightly and label it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for two to three weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller glass bottles, label them with the date and dosage instructions, which is typically 30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with hair fall because of its unique ability to promote blood circulation, strengthen hair follicles, and reduce inflammation in the scalp. The anti-inflammatory properties of stinging nettle help to soothe an itchy scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking that can cause hair loss. Additionally, the nutrient-rich formula nourishes the hair shaft, promoting healthy growth and strengthening brittle or damaged strands. As a result, stinging nettle tinctures have been shown to promote thicker, fuller hair and reduce shedding over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tincture helps reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss and thinning by soothing irritated skin and reducing redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tincture protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing breakage.

Hormone-balancing properties: Stinging nettle tincture has been shown to help balance hormones, particularly DHT (dihydrotestosterone), which is a hormone that can contribute to hair loss in both men and women by blocking its negative effects on the hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica may help with hair fall by reducing inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment that is conducive to hair growth.

Phenolic acids (including rosmarinic acid): Phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid, in Urtica dioica may help with hair fall by acting as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress on hair follicles and promoting a healthy hair growth cycle.

Sterols (including beta-sitosterol): Sterols, including beta-sitosterol, in Urtica dioica may help with hair fall by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone known to contribute to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help promote hair growth and reduce scalp irritation.
Stems: They are used because of their rich source of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C and potassium, which can help nourish and strengthen hair follicles.
Roots: They are used because of their high concentration of bioactive compounds that can help stimulate hair growth, reduce dandruff, and soothe an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its active compounds.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with hair fall because it addresses hormonal imbalances that can contribute to excessive shedding. The herb's active compound, beta-sitosterol, blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can cause hair follicles to shrink and eventually stop producing hair. By reducing DHT levels, saw palmetto tinctures promote a healthy scalp environment, allowing hair growth to thrive. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation, creating an optimal condition for hair growth and strength.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) helps reduce the production of DHT, a hormone that contributes to hair loss by shrinking hair follicles and shortening their lifespan.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss conditions such as alopecia areata, by reducing the severity of inflammation and promoting healing.
Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature hair loss and thinning.
remedy_constituents: Saw Palmetto Acid: Saw palmetto acid, a type of triterpenoid, helps with hair fall by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss.
Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, a type of iridoid, aid in hair fall prevention by exhibiting anti-androgenic properties, which can help reduce DHT levels and promote hair growth.

Furostanol Saponins: Furostanol saponins, a type of glycoside, help with hair fall by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to DHT, thereby reducing hair loss and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of lipophilic compounds, particularly beta-sitosterol, which is believed to promote hair growth and prevent hair loss.
Leaves: Utilized for their sterol content, which helps block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone known to contribute to hair loss.
Barks: Employed for their potential to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to DHT, thereby reducing hair loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh serenoa repens leaves and 2 parts of roots at dawn or before it rains.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 70% ethanol in a 1:3 ratio by weight for the infusion.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking and stirring.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and transfer the herbal tincture to a glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with hair fall because it nourishes the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The herbal extract rich in antioxidants and nutrients strengthens hair follicles, reducing the risk of breakage and shedding. Additionally, brahmi's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce stress hormones promotes a balanced hormonal system, which can also contribute to healthy hair growth. As a result, regular use of brahmi tinctures may help stimulate hair growth, thickening and strengthening existing hair while reducing the likelihood of further loss.
remedy_properties: Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of hair loss, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Antioxidant activity: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, oxidative stress, and environmental toxins, which can lead to hair loss and thinning.
Stimulating properties: Brahmi's stimulating compounds help increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a class of saponins, have been found to stimulate hair growth by increasing the length and thickness of hair follicles, thereby reducing hair fall.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair fall.

Bacopaside I: Bacopaside I, a glycoside saponin, has been found to have a potential role in regulating hair growth by modulating the expression of genes involved in hair development and reducing the formation of dioxins, which can contribute to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most widely used part due to their high bacoside content, which is believed to help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
Roots: Roots are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, contributing to hair loss.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their bacoside content and potential to stimulate hair growth, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness in hair fall treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers by cutting them with scissors when they are in full bloom.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 3 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon tinctures helps with hair fall because of its ability to promote blood circulation, stimulate hair growth, and reduce oxidative stress in the scalp. The antioxidants present in cinnamon help to combat free radicals that can damage hair follicles, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff. Additionally, cinnamon's ability to regulate blood sugar levels may also contribute to a healthy scalp environment, promoting thicker, healthier hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss by weakening hair follicles.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Ceylon cinnamon tinctures may help reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can cause hair loss by damaging hair follicles and preventing new hair growth.

Natural Stimulant of Hair Growth: Ceylon cinnamon's natural stimulatory effects on blood flow and circulation may help promote healthy hair growth by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: It helps with hair fall by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing scalp irritation and inflammation that can contribute to hair loss.
Cinnamaldehyde: It helps with hair fall by showing antioxidant properties, protecting the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
Coumarin: It helps with hair fall by exhibiting antifungal properties, reducing the growth of fungal infections on the scalp that can lead to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to promote hair growth and reduce dandruff.
Rhyzomes: Used for their antiseptic and anti-dandruff properties to soothe scalp irritations and promote healthy hair growth.
Barks: Used for their astringent properties to reduce dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp irritations that contribute to hair fall.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of high-quality cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees during the summer months.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered bark with 50% ethanol as a solvent in a ratio of 1:2 for 21 days to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that work together to nourish and strengthen the hair follicles. The tincture's ant-inflammatory properties help reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, which can cause hair loss. Additionally, its ability to promote collagen production improves hair thickness and texture, making it a natural remedy for preventing hair fall and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair Tree tincture contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and damage to hair follicles, which can contribute to hair loss.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is a common cause of hair thinning and shedding.
Stimulatory properties: Maidenhair Tree contains compounds that stimulate hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair follicles, and strengthening hair strands.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce hair fall by improving blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth, and reducing inflammation that may contribute to hair loss.

Bilobalide: A triterpene saponin found in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide has antioxidant properties that can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.

Ginkgolides: These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing hair fall.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help promote hair growth and reduce shedding.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids similar to the leaves, which may help to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Seeds: They are said to have a balancing effect on hormones, which can help to regulate hair growth and reduce hair fall caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and mature.
Clean and dry the ginkgo biloba leaves immediately to prevent mold and bacterial growth using paper bags.
Steep one part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in two parts of 80 proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material from the tincture.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut tinctures helps with hair fall because it contains aescin, a natural compound that improves blood flow to the scalp. This increased blood circulation helps to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the risk of shedding. Additionally, horse chestnut's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, further supporting overall hair health and reducing the appearance of hair loss.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a key component of horse chestnut extract, is known for its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which helps to promote healthy hair growth by increasing oxygen delivery to the scalp.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in horse chestnut tinctures has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, helping to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals and promoting a healthy hair growth environment.

Saponins: Saponins present in horse chestnut extract have been found to stimulate hair growth by increasing the length of anagen (growth) phase and reducing telogen (resting) phase, resulting in thicker and fuller hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in promoting healthy scalp circulation and reducing inflammation associated with hair loss.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and omega-3 essential fatty acids, which nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth by strengthening hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aesculus hippocastanum leaves and flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of dew.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm dark place for at least two weeks or until it reaches 10 percent moisture content.
Grind 500 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered aesculus hippocastanum with 2 parts high proof vodka and store the mixture in an amber glass bottle for six weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter after six weeks and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with hair fall because they contain rich antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote a healthy scalp and follicles. The tincture's active compounds, such as salicin and ursolic acid, help to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and strengthen the hair roots. This leads to an improvement in hair growth, reduced shedding, and thicker, fuller locks. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Kansas coneflower may also help to prevent scalp infections that can contribute to hair loss.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is a common underlying cause of hair loss and promotes healthy hair growth.
Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in the tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to hair loss, promoting a healthy and robust hair growth environment.

Immune-Modulating Properties: Kansas Coneflower's immune-modulating compounds help regulate the immune system's response to hair loss, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment conducive to hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to stimulate blood flow and promote healthy scalp conditions, contributing to a reduction in hair fall.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation, leading to healthier hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots according to your preference weighing about 100g per batch.
Chop the harvested roots into smaller pieces and combine them with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily ensuring maximum extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass bottle discarding the solids retaining only the liquid extract.
Store the final tincture in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f and use within one year keeping it away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with hair fall because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the scalp and promote healthy hair growth. The tannins present in lemon balm help to reduce dandruff and itchiness, creating an ideal environment for hair follicles to thrive. Additionally, the antioxidants in lemon balm protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, ensuring stronger and thicker hair. This natural remedy can be used topically or taken internally to promote a healthy and full head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp and hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can cause hair follicle damage and lead to hair fall.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antifungal and antibacterial properties may help combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss conditions such as dandruff and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have been traditionally used to promote hair growth and reduce hair loss due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse the plant material by gently rinsing it with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 50g of the plant material with 1000ml of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to ensure thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with hair fall because it promotes a healthy scalp environment, stimulating blood flow and reducing inflammation. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of valerian root help to combat dandruff, itchiness, and flaking, common causes of hair loss. Additionally, valerian's ability to calm the nervous system can reduce stress-related hair loss and promote a healthy growth cycle. As a result, valerian tinctures can help strengthen hair follicles, increase hair density, and promote a fuller, thicker head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian tincture contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and damage to the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing hair fall.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to hair loss and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties: Valerian tincture has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which is often linked to hair loss due to hormonal imbalances or telogen effluvium. By reducing stress, valerian may help promote a healthier balance of hormones that support hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used to make tinctures that promote healthy scalp circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help prevent hair loss.
Fruits: The fruits of the valerian plant contain antioxidants and other nutrients that help nourish the scalp and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped root material with four parts vodka in a glass jar filling it about three-quarters full.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for eight weeks allowing the mixture to macerate.
After eight weeks strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with hair fall because of its exceptional soothing and nourishing properties. The herbal extract rich in mucilage, a thick, protective coating that forms on the surface when mixed with water, gently coats and calms the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation. This leads to a healthier environment for hair growth, strengthening roots and follicles, and promoting a lush, full head of hair. Additionally, marshmallow tinctures may help stimulate blood flow and reduce stress, both common causes of hair loss.
remedy_properties: Emollient property: Marshmallow root's emollient property helps to soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to hair loss.

Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tincture forms a protective barrier on the scalp, trapping moisture and creating a favorable environment for healthy hair growth by keeping the scalp hydrated and nourished.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, which can cause hair follicles to become damaged or even shut down, leading to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make medicinal tinctures as they contain high amounts of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm scalp irritations that can contribute to hair loss.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds are rich in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, making them effective in promoting healthy hair growth by reducing inflammation and nourishing the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marshmallow roots and cut them into small pieces for 30 minutes.
Combine one part of the chopped marshmallow root with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar and shake vigorously.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years, taking 20-30 drops sublingually twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Smooth sumac tinctures helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote healthy scalp circulation. The tannins present in smooth sumac help to reduce dandruff, itchiness, and inflammation, which are common causes of hair loss. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial properties of smooth sumac help to eliminate any fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to hair fall. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, smooth sumac tinctures can help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair shedding.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sumac tincture's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress and free radicals, which can damage hair follicles and contribute to hair loss.
Inflammation reduction: Sumac has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause hair loss by damaging hair follicles and preventing new hair growth.

Stimulating blood flow: Sumac tincture's stimulating properties help improve blood circulation to the scalp, increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to hair follicles, which promotes healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help promote hair growth and reduce oxidative stress.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, a natural compound that stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried rhus glabra leaves and flowers, certified organic or wildcrafted, for maximum potency.
Combine the dried rhus glabra mixture with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar for optimal extraction.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks to allow for gradual solvation of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and preserving liquid.
Label and store the finished rhus glabra tincture at room temperature, ready for use as a dietary supplement.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with hair fall because of its rich source of silica, a mineral that plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy hair growth. Silica deficiency has been linked to brittle and fragile hair, leading to excessive shedding. The tincture's silica content nourishes the scalp and hair follicles, strengthening the hair shaft and promoting thicker, healthier locks. Additionally, horsetail's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, further supporting its ability to promote a full and luscious mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal field horsetail tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress on hair follicles, which can contribute to hair loss by protecting them from free radical damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can lead to hair fall caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or other skin irritations.

Rich in silica content: Horsetail is one of the richest natural sources of silica, an essential mineral for healthy hair growth, as it helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair's tensile strength, leading to thicker, healthier-looking locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and antioxidants, roots help promote collagen production and strengthen hair follicles.
Stems: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and phenolic acids, stems help reduce inflammation and improve scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense plant material in sufficient quantity for your desired yield.
Chop the harvested equisetum arvense into small pieces and combine with a 60% vodka solution at a ratio of 1:4.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to macerate for four weeks or longer.
After four weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles filled to the top and store in the refrigerator at 40°f or colder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with hair fall because it promotes a healthy scalp environment, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The silymarin compound found in milk thistle has been shown to stimulate hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp, reducing follicle damage, and promoting a strong, resilient hair shaft. By nourishing and protecting the hair follicles, milk thistle tinctures help to reduce shedding and promote a fuller, thicker head of hair.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk thistle's primary bioactive compound, silymarin, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair loss.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in milk thistle tinctures help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair fall and promote a healthy scalp environment.
Liver support: Milk thistle's liver-protective effects can help remove toxins that may be contributing to hair loss, promoting overall health and well-being, including healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Milk thistle roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
Seeds: The seeds of milk thistle contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect the hair follicles from damage and promotes hair regrowth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and stems of good quality in the early morning or evening.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it under running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned silybum marianum plant material into small pieces and measure out 30 grams for this recipe.
Combine the chopped plant material with 300 milliliters of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid tincture from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with hair fall because it stimulates blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The herbal extract also acts as a natural diuretic, reducing inflammation and excess sebum production that can clog pores and lead to hair loss. Additionally, dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, allowing hair to grow strong and healthy, and potentially reversing the signs of thinning or balding.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, can reduce inflammation on the scalp, which is a common underlying cause of hair loss.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content helps protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth.

Stimulating blood flow: Dandelion's active compounds, such as inulin, can stimulate blood flow to the scalp, which increases oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the hair follicles, promoting thicker, healthier hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe scalp irritation and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Contains omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin E, which nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning when they are still damp with dew.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and mix it with vodka at a ratio of 1:2 by weight for three weeks.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a dark cupboard for two to four days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass bottle and discard the solids after one hour.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place, ready for use at a dosage of 20-30 drops twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Watermelon tinctures helps with hair fall because it is rich in vitamins A, C, and B6, which nourish and strengthen the hair follicles. The antioxidants present in watermelon also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, the citrulline content in watermelon tinctures increases blood flow to the scalp, reducing the risk of hair loss caused by poor circulation. By promoting hair health from within, watermelon tinctures can help to reduce hair fall and promote thicker, fuller hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal watermelon tinctures contain antioxidants like vitamin C and lycopene, which help reduce oxidative stress on the scalp and promote healthy hair growth by neutralizing free radicals that can damage hair follicles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in watermelon tinctures, such as cucurbitacins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often a contributing factor to hair loss and thinning.

Collagen-boosting properties: Watermelon tincture contains vitamin C, which plays a crucial role in the production of collagen, a protein essential for healthy hair growth and strengthening hair follicles, reducing the risk of breakage and fall.
remedy_parts: Roots: Watermelon roots are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Watermelon seeds contain antioxidants and fatty acids that nourish the scalp and promote hair growth by strengthening hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh or dried citrullus lanatus leaves and stems in the morning on a sunny day.
Dry the citrullus lanatus material in a warm dry place for 7-10 days or use a food dehydrator at 60°c.
Grind 250g of dried citrullus lanatus into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100ml of high-proof vodka with 25g of the powdered citrullus lanatus in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to aid extraction.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hair fall are concentrated plant extracts that have been used for centuries to promote healthy hair growth and reduce hair loss. These tinctures work by nourishing the scalp, stimulating blood flow, and fortifying the hair follicles, thereby strengthening and thickening the hair. For example, peppermint oil and saw palmetto tincture can help to stimulate hair growth, while rosemary and sage tinctures promote healthy circulation and reduce dandruff. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can enjoy thicker, fuller, and healthier-looking hair, boosting their confidence and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hair fall that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and acne. Herbal tinctures like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and neem help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy healing. Other common issues addressed by herbal tinctures include digestive problems like constipation, bloating, and indigestion, as well as stress and anxiety-related conditions like insomnia and restlessness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with hair fall is a blend of saw palmetto, biotin, and rosemary. Saw palmetto helps block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss. Biotin promotes hair growth by strengthening hair follicles. Rosemary tincture improves blood circulation to the scalp, nourishing hair roots. This combination can be taken in varying ratios, such as 1 part saw palmetto, 1 part biotin, and 2 parts rosemary. Consult a healthcare professional before use.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal tinctures can be beneficial for hair fall due to their mechanism of action, which may include modulating hair loss through signaling pathways. The study reviewed experimental studies related to alopecia and identified 108 herbal combinations, with Angelica, Rehmannia glutinosa, Ligusticum chuanxiong hort, and Radix Rehmanniae being among the most commonly used herbs. Among these combinations, a high association with vascular drugs was found, suggesting that herbal tinctures may help improve hair growth by enhancing blood circulation and promoting hair follicle health.
